top_signals.title: Oil Falls as Iran Peace Deal Gets Tankers Moving Through Hormuz
top_signals.summary: Oil fell and gasoline sank after the US and Iran agreed to settle a months-long conflict that triggered the biggest supply disruption in history, and some vessels were seen making their first crossings of the Strait of Hormuz since the war began.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Warsh's Debut Sparks Surge in Hike Bets
top_signals.summary: Traders piled into betting on interest-rate hikes as soon as next month after Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh clear the central bank won’t tolerate high inflation. The hawkish message was driven home by the projections of individual Fed members. Bloomberg's Alister Bull reports. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: China’s tribute system and the new world order
top_signals.summary: Exercising power without resorting to war could be Beijing’s ultimate show of strength
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
top_signals.summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Aldi is making a $9bn bet that it can finally crack America
top_signals.summary: After 50 years of slow US expansion the German discount grocer is upping the pace. Can it win over hard-pressed consumers?
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Amazon AI exec predicts first 'commercially useful' quantum computers in 5-7 years
top_signals.summary: Quantum computing is becoming an increasingly competitive field, with tech giants including Microsoft, Google and IBM developing the technology.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Meet the keepers of the UK’s locks
top_signals.summary: The Canal & River Trust stretches 4,700 miles – 8,000 volunteers have got it covered

top_signals.title: Five ways to celebrate Pride this summer
The Diesel Demand Shock Nobody Is Pricing In
top_signals.summary: The Truck Exception Isn't as Big as You Think When I recently argued that the internal combustion engine may already have passed its peak, many readers responded with a familiar objection. Cars are one thing, they argued, but trucks are different. Passenger vehicles can be electrified because they drive predictable distances, return home every evening, and increasingly benefit from lower operating costs. Long-haul trucking, by contrast, depends on heavy payloads, long distances, and energy densities that batteries supposedly cannot provide. While…

top_signals.title: France to stop certifying products lacking quantum-resistant encryption
top_signals.summary: France’s cybersecurity agency plans to block certification of products without quantum-resistant encryption starting in 2027, with full adoption targeted for 2030.
